message:
test(readme): readme_time_series_operations_compiles_and_runs (fd90.178 / frankenpandas-9t5qk)

Implements the integration test registered in
frankenpandas-9t5qk. Mirrors the README's "Time-Series Operations"
section (lines 1256-1276) end-to-end so a regression in any of the
documented user-facing APIs surfaces as a test failure rather than
broken docs.

Coverage:

- DataFrame::at_time("12:00:00")? on a frame indexed by ISO datetime
  strings — proves the string-typed time matcher round-trips.
- DataFrame::between_time("09:00:00", "12:00:00")? — proves the
  inclusive-on-both-ends window contract that the README example
  relies on.
- Series::dt() accessor: year, month, day, hour, minute, second,
  dayofweek, dayofyear, quarter, weekofyear, is_month_start,
  is_month_end, is_quarter_start, is_quarter_end, strftime(fmt).
  All called on a 3-row Series with a deliberately tricky payload
  (leap day, end-of-year second, mid-day) so the strftime row alone
  catches both date- and time-component drift.
- Timezone operations: dt().tz_localize(Some("America/New_York"))?
  — produces an already-localized series, which is then chained
  into dt().tz_convert(Some("UTC"))?. Order matters because the
  README example calls tz_convert on output that is already
  localised; calling it on a naive series would surface a
  different (and uninteresting) error.

Pure additive test; uses prelude-only imports per the existing
file convention. No source/library changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
